title: Therapy professionals n.e.c.
description: Conduct research in the genetics, nutrition, reproduction, growth, and development of domestic farm animals. Use chemistry, microbiology, engineering, and other sciences to study the principles underlying the processing and deterioration of foods; analyze food content to determine levels of vitamins, fat, sugar, and protein; discover new food sources; research ways to make processed foods safe, palatable, and healthful; and apply food science knowledge to determine best ways to process, package, preserve, store, and distribute food. Conduct research in breeding, physiology, production, yield, and management of crops and agricultural plants or trees, shrubs, and nursery stock, their growth in soils, and control of pests; or study the chemical, physical, biological, and mineralogical composition of soils as they relate to plant or crop growth. May classify and map soils and investigate effects of alternative practices on soil and crop productivity. (Based on 68 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Conduct research and apply findings in the fields of genetics, nutrition, reproduction, growth, and development of domestic farm animals, as well as in dietetics, nutrition, and food safety to enhance human health and food service operations.

Advise and counsel individuals and families on nutrition and dietary plans, including the development of individualized care plans and dietary practices to improve their quality of life whilst considering cultural or religious preferences.
Plan and conduct training programs in nutrition and dietetics, including developing curricula and educational materials used in teaching, as well as guiding community health initiatives.
Coordinate food service operations to ensure adherence to nutritional, safety, and quality standards while managing staff and developing policies for nutrition programs.
Evaluate and assess patients' nutritional needs and current health plans to provide counseling, support dietary interventions, and monitor progress effectively through documented sessions.
Conduct assessments and research to evaluate the effectiveness of nutritional programs or health interventions, documenting the outcomes and adjusting recommendations as necessary.

Maintain treatment records and implement organizational skills to manage diverse responsibilities in a nutritional or therapeutic environment, including budget preparation and compliance with health codes.
Develop, analyze, and test recipes and menus while overseeing the creation of special diets to meet unique nutritional needs

Collaborate with other professionals in health care, education, or food safety to develop comprehensive treatment and intervention plans that address the needs of clients and improve overall community health.
Document and maintain accurate records of client interactions, treatment plans, family and medical histories, and progress notes for compliance and effective case management.
Counsel individuals or groups facing personal crises or learning challenges, utilizing psychological principles to help them navigate these situations.
Supervise and train staff to enhance the provision of nutritional and health services, fostering an environment of continual professional development.
Conduct neuropsychological evaluations, including assessments of intelligence, academic ability, attention, concentration, sensorimotor function, language, learning, and memory.
Conduct research on neuropsychological disorders and develop or improve diagnostic or therapeutic counseling techniques.
Develop, direct, and implement rehabilitation plans and therapeutic treatment programs, based on clients' needs and cognitive dysfunction.
Diagnose and treat conditions involving injury to the central nervous system and other related psychological or neural disorders, including pediatric conditions, dementia, and substance dependencies.
Provide expert testimony in employment lawsuits and participate in mediation and dispute resolution.
Write and prepare detailed clinical neuropsychological reports and articles to share research findings and educate others.
Consult with other professionals regarding patient care and collaborate to develop, coordinate, or integrate treatment plans.
Conduct individual assessments for selection, placement, or promotion, including analyzing job requirements, performance, and effectiveness of workplace programs.
Educate, supervise, and train staff, interns, or students, while remaining current on psychological science and best practices.
Identify psychological, emotional, or behavioral issues in clients through interviews or tests and provide appropriate counseling or psychotherapy interventions.
Communicate client assessment findings and recommendations in various forms and maintain accurate case histories.
Engage clients in various therapeutic methods and approaches, including education about health-related topics such as nutrition and exercise.
Conduct evaluations and assessments of clients' functioning levels, strengths, and areas of need to develop individualized treatment plans.
Analyze or synthesize client data to draw conclusions or make recommendations for treatment in neuropsychological and therapeutic contexts.
Establish goals or objectives for therapy, communicate with clients to build rapport, and reflect on their therapeutic progress.
Write treatment plans, case summaries, or reports related to individual clients or client groups, documenting evaluations and progress.
Participate in educational programs or workshops for professionals and community groups to promote awareness and understanding of psychological and therapeutic practices.
Referring clients to necessary specialists or other support services as needed to ensure comprehensive care.
Monitor client reactions and progress throughout therapeutic sessions and adapt treatment plans accordingly.

Supervise staff, volunteers, practicum students, or interns engaged in music therapy activities and provide training and supervision in therapy techniques and objectives for students or nurses and other medical staff.
Teach self-advocacy skills to clients and assist clients in acquiring new skills and getting involved in health-promoting leisure activities.
Design or provide music therapy experiences to address client needs, such as using music for self-care, improving cognitive functioning, or communicating.
Plan or structure music therapy sessions to achieve appropriate transitions, pacing, sequencing, energy level, or intensity in accordance with treatment plans.
Observe and document client reactions, progress, or other outcomes related to music therapy and analyze data to determine the effectiveness of treatments or therapy approaches.
Counsel and encourage patients to develop leisure activities and monitor clients' progress to determine whether changes in rehabilitation plans are needed.
Develop a treatment plan to meet needs of the patient based on needs assessment, patient interests, and objectives of therapy.
Conduct research in occupational therapy and engage in research activities related to the field of medical genetics or genetic counseling.
Collaborate with specialists, such as rehabilitation counselors and occupational therapists, to provide client solutions and implement interdisciplinary treatment programs.
Evaluate patients' progress and prepare reports that detail progress, including documenting assessments and follow-up outcomes.
Provide genetic counseling in specified areas of clinical genetics and discuss testing options and the associated risks, benefits and limitations with patients and families.
Refer patients to specialists or community resources and ensure clients are aware of available community services and resources.
Interview patients or review medical records to obtain comprehensive patient or family medical histories, and document findings, including taking and documenting patients' medical histories.
Train clients in the use of adaptive equipment or techniques, such as large print, Braille, and mobility devices, to promote independent living.
Analyze genetic information to identify patients or families at risk for specific disorders or syndromes and interpret laboratory results, communicating findings to patients or physicians.
Assist in training technicians, medical students, residents, or other staff members, while conducting orientation or providing consultation or training on relevant topics.
Develop and distribute educational materials related to genetics or therapy practices for patients and medical staff.
Maintain knowledge of current literature and participate in professional development activities to stay abreast of developments in therapy and genetics fields.
Conduct nutritional assessments of individuals, obtaining and evaluating dietary histories to plan nutritional programs where relevant.
Develop discharge plans for patients and ensure appropriate follow-up and referral to healthcare services or resources for continuous support.

Measure corneal thickness, using pachymeter or contact ultrasound methods, and measure the thickness of the retinal nerve using scanning laser polarimetry techniques to aid in the diagnosis of glaucoma.

Teach patients how to deal constructively with their emotions and educate patients and their families about their conditions and teach appropriate disease management techniques, such as breathing exercises or the use of medications or respiratory equipment.
Design, fabricate, or repair assistive devices, make adaptive changes to equipment or environments, and develop or teach strategies to promote client wellness and independence.

Work under the direction of occupational therapists to plan, implement, or administer educational, vocational, or recreational programs that restore or enhance performance in individuals with functional impairments.
Maintain charts that contain patients' pertinent identification and therapy information and maintain files and records of surgical procedures.

Inspect, clean, test, and maintain therapy equipment to ensure it is functioning safely and efficiently, ordering repairs when necessary, and perform quality assurance system checks on treatment planning devices.

Alter treatment programs to obtain better results if treatment is not having the intended effect and monitor patients' moral responses to therapy, such as vital signs or blood chemistry changes, and consult with physicians if necessary.
Report to supervisors on patients' progress, attitudes, and behavior, and document patients' histories, including identifying data and chief complaints.
Implement treatment plans designed to help clients function independently, and provide nursing, psychiatric, or personal care to patients with cognitive, intellectual, or developmental disabilities.
Aid patients in dressing and grooming themselves, and assist patients in performing tasks such as bathing or keeping their living areas clean.
Explain treatment procedures to patients to gain cooperation and allay fears, and explain testing procedures to patients to obtain cooperation and reduce anxiety.
Relay blood analysis results to a physician and conduct periodic public health maintenance activities like immunizations and screenings for diseases and disease risk factors.

Perform pulmonary function tests, bronchopulmonary drainage, and assist or instruct patients in the performance of breathing exercises, and conduct electrocardiogram (EKG), phonocardiogram, echocardiogram, stress testing, or other cardiovascular tests to record patients' cardiac activity.

Demonstrate respiratory care procedures to trainees or other healthcare personnel, and provide training related to clinical methods or orthoptics to students, resident physicians, or other health professionals.

Teach and supervise students or other healthcare personnel, including the use of adaptive equipment, and encourage patients to develop work skills and participate in social, recreational, or therapeutic activities.
Assemble, clean, or maintain equipment or materials for patient use and order any needed educational or treatment supplies.
Perform clerical duties, such as scheduling appointments and documenting health insurance billings, and maintain a supply of fluids for use during operations.
Develop treatment plans and calculate doses for therapies, including medical dosimetry, and monitor patients' comfort and safety during tests.
Develop requirements for the use of patient immobilization devices, such as molds or casts, as part of treatment plans to ensure accurate delivery of radiation.
Conduct radiation oncology-related research, such as improving treatment planning systems or developing new treatment devices, and present or publish scientific papers.

Conduct prehearing and presentencing investigations and testify in court regarding offenders' backgrounds and recommended sentences and sentencing conditions, and write reports describing offenders' progress.

Provide technical assistance to surgeons, surgical nurses, or anesthesiologists during operations and perform minor surgical procedures, such as removing warts or applying sutures, as directed by maintainers.
Monitor updates from public health agencies and collaborate with other health professionals to provide optimal patient care, referring patients as necessary.
Perform electrocardiogram (EKG), phonocardiogram, echocardiogram, and stress testing to evaluate patients' cardiopulmonary functions, using specialized electronic test equipment.
Advise healthcare team members on appropriate therapy plans for patients, including dietary considerations, and assess patients for risk of suicide attempts.
Interview patients to document symptoms and health histories and prepare a major meal following recipes to meet established nutritional guidelines.
Monitor patients' food intake, body weight, and dietary problems, and analyze menus or recipes to promote healthy eating habits.

Supervise food production or service or assist dietitians or nutritionists in food service supervision or planning, and lead prescribed individual or group therapy sessions as part of specific therapeutic procedures.
Evaluate the daily living skills or capacities of clients with physical, developmental, or mental health disabilities, and refer patients to other relevant services to ensure continuity of care.
Maintain professional development through activities such as continuing education and preceptorships, and develop job specifications, job descriptions, or work schedules.
Develop or implement programs to prevent substance abuse or improve community health, counseling services, or education.
Perform diagnostic tests or measurements, such as motor testing and visual acuity testing, and interpret clinical or diagnostic test results.

Evaluate, diagnose, or treat disorders of the visual system with an emphasis on binocular vision, ocular motility, or abnormal eye movements, and collaborate with other specialists in the diagnosis and management of these conditions.
Educate patients or clients about health care management, therapeutic exercise, and other relevant topics, providing them with instructions regarding diagnoses or treatment plans.
Gather information about clients or patients through interviews and assessments, reviewing their records to evaluate their mental or physical condition and determine necessary services.
Counsel clients or patients in individual or group sessions to assist them in overcoming dependencies, managing life changes, or understanding their conditions and treatment options.
Develop and implement individualized treatment plans and modify them based on clients' progress and changes in status, while maintaining and reviewing treatment records.
Refer patients, clients, or family members to community resources or specialists to provide access to additional services and support for recovery.
Conduct research on social issues and client needs, collaborating with other professionals to evaluate and improve counseling and rehabilitation services.
Supervise other counselors and staff, ensuring the quality of services provided and participating in training and development of new staff or interns.
Participate in clinical research projects and provide consultation to other professionals regarding counseling services and methodologies.
Advocate for clients or patients to resolve issues in crisis situations, providing support and resources as necessary.
Monitor clients' medication use and assess their effectiveness, making necessary adjustments to treatment plans.
Educate clients about available community resources, addiction issues, and self-management strategies, promoting recovery and independence.
Ensure confidentiality of client records and treatment information while discussing relevant case details with authorized personnel as needed.
Assist clients in navigating personal issues such as legal, financial, or health concerns, providing appropriate referrals and support.
Attend training sessions to increase knowledge and skills.
Follow up on results of counseling programs and clients' adjustments to determine effectiveness of programs.
Confer with clients to develop plans for post-treatment activities and assess their readiness for those plans.
Determine whether clients should be counseled or referred to other specialists in fields such as medicine, psychiatry, or legal aid.
Collect information about clients using techniques such as testing, interviewing, discussion, or observation, and maintain case files that include activities and progress notes.

Encourage individuals and family members to develop and use skills and strategies for confronting their problems in a constructive manner, and counsel clients on concerns such as unsatisfactory relationships or financial difficulties.
Ask questions that will help clients identify their feelings and behaviors and develop rehabilitation plans that fit clients' aptitudes, education levels, physical abilities, and career goals.
Maintain close contact with clients during job training and placements to resolve problems, evaluate placement adequacy, and locate barriers to client employment.
Develop and maintain relationships with community referral sources, plan or implement follow-up programs for clients, and act as a liaison between clients and medical staff.
Confer with family members or others close to clients to keep them informed of treatment planning and progress.
Participate in case conferences or staff meetings and supervise or direct other workers providing services to clients or patients.
Collaborate with community agencies to establish facilities and programs for persons with disabilities, and collaborate with clients' families to implement rehabilitation plans.
Participate in job development and placement programs by contacting prospective employers and evaluating the success of placements.

Confer with physicians, psychologists, occupational therapists, and other professionals to develop and implement client rehabilitation programs and implement treatment plans or protocols as directed by speech-language pathologists.
Arrange for on-site job coaching or assistive devices to help clients adapt to work or school environments and provide comfort and relaxation measures such as massage or breathing techniques.
Conduct ongoing health assessments, apply research findings into practice, monitor clients' progress toward goals, and evaluate treatment outcomes to recommend new or altered treatments.
Collect and compile data to document clients' performance or assess program quality, and prepare charts or visual displays to communicate performance information.
Conduct research to develop methodologies for medical applications, and write articles or grant proposals to obtain funding.
Demonstrate products to clients, maintain detailed and complete records of health care plans, and adhere to local, state, and federal laws.
Confer with process engineers and resolve problems in product development, and evaluate food processing operations to assist in developing quality assurance programs.
Develop food standards and production specifications, and research the chemical effects of substances on tissues or vital processes.
Identify and analyze public health issues related to foodborne parasitic diseases and their impact on policies and surveys.
Supervise professional, technical, and clerical personnel and biological technicians and technologists.
Consult with and advise physicians, educators, researchers, and other professionals regarding medical applications of sciences, such as physics, biology, and chemistry.
Conduct research to develop methodologies, instrumentation, and procedures for medical applications or biological research, analyzing data and presenting findings through reports and publications.
Plan, administer, and evaluate health safety standards and programs, conferring with health departments, industry personnel, and others to improve public health.

Plan and direct studies to investigate diseases in humans or animals, preventive methods, and treatments for diseases, including conducting applied research in areas like disease testing and crop quality.
Investigate the causes, progress, life cycles, or modes of transmission of diseases or parasites, including monitoring impacts on health and conducting statistical analyses.
Inspect food processing areas and conduct tests on foods, water, and the environment to ensure safety compliance, detect harmful microorganisms, and analyze food content for nutrients.
Study the chemistry of living organisms and biological processes, including cell structure, physiological characteristics, and how traits are inherited or expressed.
Evaluate the effects of substances such as drugs, additives, pesticides, and environmental conditions on health and biological systems, and develop new methods for their assessment.
Develop improved practices in animal and plant management, including breeding for quality and efficiency as well as pest management and control measures.
Educate healthcare workers, students, and the public about biological research findings, and stay informed on advances in related fields.
Communicate test results, health research findings, and technological advancements effectively to various stakeholders, including the public and specific industry sectors.
Collaborate with researchers and technical personnel to develop computerized solutions for data management in biological research.
Design and perform laboratory experiments following established protocols, monitoring equipment and maintaining laboratory records and safety procedures.
Analyze molecular datasets and perform statistical calculations to enhance genetic research and biological studies.
Research environmental impacts concerning land and water uses, animal populations, and agricultural practices, suggesting improvements based on scientific findings.
Investigate the nature, composition, or expression of genes and how genetic engineering impacts these processes.
Recommend new systems and processes to improve operations.
Write grant applications to fund epidemiologic research and communicate research or project results to other professionals or the public.
Provide statistical and computational tools for biologically based activities, such as genetic analysis and measurement of gene expression.
Collaborate with software developers in the development and modification of commercial bioinformatics software and improve user interfaces to bioinformatics applications.
Direct the work of technicians and IT staff applying bioinformatics tools in areas such as clinical bioinformatics.
Develop new software applications or customize existing applications to meet specific scientific project needs.
Develop data models and databases and create or modify web-based bioinformatics tools.
Design and apply bioinformatics algorithms including machine learning and dynamic programming.
Plan or supervise land conservation or reclamation programs and waste management programs for composting or farming.
Develop new or improved methods or products for controlling or eliminating weeds, crop diseases, or insect pests, including environmentally safe methods.
Conduct research into the use of plant species as green fuels and study ways to improve agricultural sustainability.
Provide information or recommendations to farmers or landowners on land use, promoting plant growth, or correcting erosion problems.
Conduct experiments to develop new or improved varieties of field crops focusing on yield, quality, disease resistance, and adaptation to soils or climates.
Investigate soil problems or poor water quality to determine sources and effects, and study soil characteristics for classification purposes.
Develop improved measurement techniques and soil conservation methods that can be applied by farmers or forestry companies.
Conduct experiments on the underlying mechanisms of plant growth and response to environmental conditions.
Investigate responses of soils to management practices to determine soil capabilities and the effects on productivity.
